---
title: "The Road to 21.1km: A Data-Driven Training Journey"
description: "How I tracked my training volume and performance to complete my first half-marathon."
author: "Matteo"
date: "2025-04-15"
image: "image-test.jpg" 
categories: [Running, Data Analysis, Python]
code-fold: true
jupyter: python3
---

## The Challenge

In September, I set myself a goal: to run a half-marathon by the end of April. As someone new to long-distance running, I wanted to document my progress and see how my training volume directly impacted my performance in intermediate races.

## Training Volume Evolution

The following interactive chart shows my weekly training volume (in hours) from the start of my journey in September to the race month in April. 

```{python}
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

# Creating the dataset based on my training logs
data = {
    'Month': ['Sept', 'Oct', 'Nov', 'Dec', 'Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr'],
    'Hours per Week': [1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 4, 0, 7],
    'Phase': ['Beginning', 'Baseline', '5km Prep', 'Building', '10km Prep', 'Peak', 'Slump', 'Final Push']
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Creating an interactive bar chart with Plotly
fig = px.bar(df, x='Month', y='Hours per Week', 
             title='Weekly Training Volume (Sept - Apr)',
             text='Hours per Week',
             hover_data=['Phase'],
             color='Hours per Week',
             color_continuous_scale='Viridis')

fig.update_traces(texttemplate='%{text}h', textposition='outside')
fig.update_layout(yaxis_title="Volume (Hours/Week)", xaxis_title="Timeline")

fig.show()
```

Analysis of the Slump
As shown in the chart, March was a difficult month. Between a heavy workload at university and a temporary loss of motivation, my training volume dropped to zero.

However, realizing the deadline was approaching, I managed a "Final Push" in April, peaking at 7 hours of training per week. This recovery was crucial to build the necessary endurance for the 21.1km distance.

Milestones & Results
Throughout this journey, I participated in two intermediate races to test my fitness:

November: Completed a 5km in 28 minutes.

January: Completed a 10km in 57 minutes.

Final Performance: The Half-Marathon
On April 15th, I finally crossed the finish line of my first half-marathon with a time of 1:53. Despite the inconsistent training in March, the high-intensity preparation in April allowed me to maintain a steady pace and exceed all my limits

Conclusion
Using Python to visualize my training helped me stay accountable. Seeing the "zero" in March visually represented on the chart was a wake-up call that motivated the final success of this project.
